aboutMubasa: MUBASA is the Makerere University Business School Academic Staff Association — the representative body for academic and administrative staff at MUBS. Members include Teaching Assistants, Assistant Lecturers, Lecturers, Senior Lecturers, Associate Professors, and Professors across Main Campus (Nakawa), Arua, Mbarara, Jinja, and Mbale.
aboutMubs: Makerere University Business School (MUBS) is a constituent college of Makerere University. The school motto is Enabling the Future. MUBASA exists to represent staff interests in welfare, governance, promotions, working conditions, and collective advocacy with management and through FASPU at national level.

campaignCandidate.name: Ssendi Samuel
campaignCandidate.summary: Ssendi Samuel is an academic and researcher at MUBS applying machine learning to public health. He brings policy knowledge, institutional governance experience, and a practical delivery-focused approach. His manifesto is grounded in the MUBS HR Manual 2024, Universities Act Cap 210, Public Service Standing Orders 2021, FASPU-PUNTSEF Collective Agreements, and the MUBS Strategic Plan 2025–2030.

endorsedChairperson.name: Mr. Arinda Albert
endorsedChairperson.summary: Albert Arinda is the endorsed Chairperson candidate on this campaign ticket. He brings leadership experience and a commitment to a united, delivery-focused MUBASA executive.

campaignDuo.title: The Arinda–Ssendi ticket
campaignDuo.message: Albert Arinda for Chairperson and Ssendi Samuel for Deputy Chairperson form a strong leadership duo for MUBASA 2026–2028. Arinda provides executive leadership and institutional direction; Ssendi brings policy depth, systems thinking, and operational follow-through. Together they offer balanced, complementary leadership — vision at the top and delivery in the deputy role — to serve every MUBASA member at every rank and campus.
campaignDuo.recommendation: Members are encouraged to vote Albert Arinda for Chairperson and Ssendi Samuel for Deputy Chairperson as a complementary team.

manifestoCommitment: I am not here to reinvent MUBASA. I am here to make it work better for every member. The policies are in place. The strategic plan is on the table. The management has shown willingness to engage. What is needed now is an association that follows through, keeps records, and reports back honestly. That is the job. I am ready for it. I humbly ask for your vote. — Ssendi Samuel

manifestoPillars.title: Unity
manifestoPillars.tagline: One voice. Every rank. Every faculty. Every campus.
manifestoPillars.intro: MUBASA is strongest when its members move as one body — every faculty, every rank, every campus (Main, Arua, Mbarara, Jinja, Mbale). Teaching Assistants through Professors, all represented equally.
commitments: Strengthen MUBASA's formal engagement with management for collective, documented staff positions.
Represent members individually on promotion, classification, and workplace issues.
Continue FASPU engagement for national-level advocacy.
Extend communication and activities to regional campuses.
Ensure every member knows their rights under the HR Manual, Universities Act, and Standing Orders.
Stand behind members facing intimidation or harassment through existing grievance procedures.

manifestoPillars.title: Welfare
manifestoPillars.tagline: Real healthcare. Better working conditions. Science Pay.
manifestoPillars.intro: Progress on staff welfare deserves acknowledgement — salary harmonisation in 2024, RBS reinstated, medical insurance announced. MUBASA must push gains further and address remaining gaps.
commitments: Push salary harmonisation for mid-level staff through FASPU.
Correct science vs arts salary classification anomaly at MUBS.
Ensure timely RBS and NSSF remittance.
Fast-track operationalisation of medical insurance for staff and dependants.
Support Health Centre digitisation and mental health in the welfare package.
Improve staff transport, office space, and internet infrastructure.

manifestoPillars.title: Growth
manifestoPillars.tagline: Fair promotions. Research support. Professional development.
manifestoPillars.intro: Staff growth is tied to MUBS Strategic Plan 2025–2030 Human Capital pillar. MUBASA must ensure that commitment becomes real opportunity for every member.
commitments: Published annual promotions calendar with clear timelines.
Transparent promotion process per HR Manual criteria.
Equitable research funding and structured mentorship across faculties.
Transparent study leave and fair bonding terms.
A digital MUBASA member platform for documents, welfare info, and updates.

manifestoPillars.title: Sustainability
manifestoPillars.tagline: An organised, accountable association that delivers.
manifestoPillars.intro: MUBASA will outlast this term. Sustainability means a stronger, better organised, more trusted association for future members.
commitments: Consistent general meetings with minutes published within two weeks.
Member feedback channel for all ranks.
Documented handover for incoming executives.
Transparent financial reporting at AGMs.
Partner in delivering MUBS Strategic Plan 2025–2030 Human Capital pillar.

mubsLeadership.note: Prof. Moses Muhwezi is the Principal of Makerere University Business School (MUBS). He succeeded Prof. Wasswa Balunywa and is the second person to hold the Principal role in MUBS history. Prof. Rachel Mindra Katoroogo serves as Acting Deputy Principal — the first woman to hold this position at MUBS.

strategicPlan2025_2030.title: MUBS Strategic Plan 2025–2030
strategicPlan2025_2030.theme: Transforming into a Global Innovative Institution for Business and Management Education, Research and Training.
strategicPlan2025_2030.launched: 4 March 2026 at the MUBS Digital Library. Keynote address by Principal Prof. Moses Muhwezi.
strategicPlan2025_2030.mottoAlignment: Aligns with the MUBS motto "Enabling the Future."
strategicPlan2025_2030.principalEmphasis: Prof. Muhwezi highlighted fostering a culture of innovation and entrepreneurial thinking, strengthening industry partnerships for practical learning, academic excellence, and producing graduates equipped for the modern workplace.

pillars.name: Teaching, Learning and Student Success
highlights: Introduction of full online academic programmes
Integration of degree and vocational skills
Strengthened academic support to improve pass rates

pillars.name: Infrastructure and Digital Transformation
highlights: Construction of an on-campus training hotel

pillars.name: Research, Innovation and Community Engagement
highlights: Support to national programmes including PDM, Tenfold, and ATMS

pillars.name: Equity and Social Safeguards
highlights: Institutionalised CSR initiatives including the Luzira inmates programme
Increased access for persons with disabilities and disadvantaged groups

pillars.name: Human Capital and Sustainability
highlights: Strategic staff development and succession planning
Establishment of a commercial arm of MUBS
Resource mobilisation through alumni engagement and an endowment fund
pillars.mubasaLink: Central to MUBASA manifesto Pillars 03 (Growth) and 04 (Sustainability). MUBASA should monitor and support management delivery of staff development commitments.

pillars.name: Partnerships and Internationalisation
highlights: Implementation of the MUBS marketing and branding strategy

strategicPlan2025_2030.sources: MUBS launch communications, MUBS News, Independent magazine, UG Standard
